closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_inventory.summary: Run the tracked test-mesh owner and child-evidence tests.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Define parent gate, routine/release scope, split trigger, and required validation/cell/shard ids.
Derive child suites from a FlowGuard validation model and inventory ownership, status, freshness, skips, results, and reuse proof.
Map every partition/cell/shard to a registered child and assess current final artifacts for the requested scope.
Run TestMesh checks and reject hidden skips/timeouts, progress-only/background results, stale reuse, or unowned required cells.
Return parent confidence only from current child evidence and typed MTA/process/risk handoffs.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Govern parent/child validation ownership, cell/shard coverage, final result artifacts, freshness, reuse, and routine/release scope.

do_not_use_when: The task is ordinary small tests, semantic obligation definition, test execution itself, or a production code/model split.
routes.summary: Govern parent/child validation ownership, cell/shard coverage, final result artifacts, freshness, reuse, and routine/release scope.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-test-mesh skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Accept a parent gate and model-derived child split; review child ownership/status/freshness; block hidden skip/timeout/progress/release gaps or unowned cells; hand semantic bindings and lif.
description: Use when tests, checks, transition cells, payload cases, or evidence are large, slow, layered, stale, skipped, backgrounded, release-only, or require parent/child suite ownersh.
Route id: test_mesh_maintenance; role: public_owner; native owner: test_mesh_maintenance. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns validation hierarchy, not semantic obligations or te.
Use for large/slow/background validation, child test scripts, stale/reused evidence, release-only gates, or transition/artifact-payload matrices.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Background progress is liveness only; reuse requires current TestResultReuseTicket and ProofArtifactRef.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard validates test-mesh governance and cannot turn liveness into native pass evidence.
Every required cell/shard needs a registered current passing owner; missing target derivation, hidden skips, or template harvest closure blocks broad parent confidence.
Govern parent/child test hierarchy, explicit validation partitions, result artifacts, and evidence freshness without inlining every child test.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
name: flowguard-test-mesh
Read references/test_mesh_protocol.md for target split derivation, ownership/evidence rows, reuse proof, transition/payload matrices, and routine/release scope.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus a validation mesh diagram and child freshness.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when tests, checks, transition cells, payload cases, or evidence are large, slow, layered, stale, skipped, backgrounded.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not split production code/models, decide semantic obligations, run tests directly, or treat release-only/background progress as routine proof; return small ordinary tests to model-first-.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Define the parent gate and derive target child suites/scripts from a FlowGuard validation-structure model.
Map partitions/cells/shards to owners and attach status, freshness, proof artifacts, reuse tickets, skips, timeouts, and result paths.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Review routine/release scope and return current child evidence plus Model-Test Alignment, process, and risk handoffs.
Required suites/cells/shards have current final pass artifacts, visible skips, and valid reuse proof when reused.
Child suites/scripts derive from a named validation-structure model and cover the parent partitions.
Running/background progress, release-only evidence in routine scope, stale, skipped, timeout, or missing child results cannot satisfy parent confidence.
